title: leucas urticifolia var. urticifolia
intro: Leucas urticifolia var. urticifolia, also known as the wild sage or Indian sage, is a plant species that belongs to the mint family and is native to India and other parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cold, and skin infections. The health benefits of Leucas urticifolia var. urticifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can also be used as a topical application to treat skin conditions and wounds.
